headline: Lockheed Martin vs. Boeing: Which Industrials Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
summary: Lockheed Martin leans on defense contracts and steady margins, while Boeing eyes a commercial rebound after a dramatic revenue surge in 2025.

headline: Lockheed Martin Javelin Launcher Delivery Highlights Valuation Gap And Growth Watchpoints
summary: Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) and Raytheon’s Javelin Joint Venture delivered the first Lightweight Command Launch Units to the U.S. Army. The Lightweight Command Launch Unit is described as a next generation launcher with reduced size and weight and updated performance for the Javelin system. This delivery marks a shift from prior updates on factory output and munitions capacity toward fielded hardware in the hands of end users. For investors watching NYSE:LMT, this milestone sits in the core...

headline: SCHD’s $71.6 Billion Portfolio Delivers 20% Gains Plus 3.3% Yield This Year
summary: Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (NYSEARCA:SCHD) has become one of the most-owned dividend funds in the country, with $71.6 billion in assets and a 0.06% expense ratio. SCHD currently yields roughly 3.3% and has delivered a 20% year-to-date price return on top of income, outpacing the S&P 500. The key question is whether the distribution ... headline: RTX (RTX)’s The Javelin Joint Venture Makes its First LWCLU Delivery
summary: RTX Corporation (NYSE:RTX) is one of the 10 Best Stocks in Steve Cohen’s Portfolio. On May 26, 2026, The Javelin Joint Venture, a partnership between Raytheon, a subsidiary of RTX Corporation (NYSE:RTX), and Lockheed Martin (LMT), delivered its first Lightweight Command Launch Units (LWCLU) to the U.S. Army. headline: U.S. Scores Major Rare Earth Win With Greenland Deposit Deal
summary: REalloys secures a 15-year offtake deal for heavy rare earths from Greenland's Tanbreez project, adding critical dysprosium and terbium supply ahead of the Pentagon's 2027 China ban.
